title: Carboxylic acids
summary: 9701 AS carboxylic acid chemistry — structure, weak acidity, reactions, and hydrogen bonding with PhET molecule builder.
learningObjectives: Describe the carboxyl group and name carboxylic acids.
Explain weak acid behaviour and Ka values.
Predict reactions with bases, carbonates, and alcohols.
Account for boiling points via intermolecular hydrogen bonding.

simpleExplanation.title: The –COOH group
simpleExplanation.summary: Carboxylic acids are weak acids because the carboxylate anion is stabilised by resonance. They form dimers through strong H-bonds.
steps: With NaOH → salt + water; with Na₂CO₃ → salt + CO₂ + H₂O.
With alcohol + acid catalyst → ester + water.
Oxidation of primary alcohols gives carboxylic acids.

sections.content: **Carboxylic acids** (18.1) feed into **esters** (18.2) and **acyl chlorides** (A Level). Show **curly arrows** for C–O bond breaking when reacting with nucleophiles.
